Ferpa Access

For posterity.

Exact copy of common app and SAT Score Report.

Beginning of Evaluation:
Page 1:
Andrew Tsai
Male
Asian
03/18/1995

Official Decision: Admit Cmte (committee) Decision: Admit Reader Decision: Admit App Type: RD BME Decision: BME Admit

Rigor: M V D A L Caclulus Taken: Calculus Grade:

1st Comment (redacted title), 02/01/2013: Calc(10th), Bio, Lang, Stats, Phys, Gov, Japanese = 7 (they missed chem, possibly b/c i didn’t take the class) 2nd Comment (redacted title), 02/02/2013:
Eagle Scout, several i’ships and comp. (Med Cntr, Stanford research on sustainability), JSA(4-P), JNHS(4-VP), Public Forum(4) Essay 1 - on eczema, summary, lived independently in SF Essay 2 - interested in “new medical solns”, summary C(hoir) Rec - “cool… a leader b/c leads by example” Music T Rec - humor, quick to laugh, creative Lang T Rec - exhuberant, insightful Sci T - notes dedication, intellectual engagement

A lot to like here! Andy emerges as a tough student + engaged participant in the world around him (though he has done well in numerous sci related comp, he’s a JSA member and an Eagle Scout too a bit more diverse than the standard). Add to this terrific metrics, a substantive interest in engr(not MD-bound) + some of the most engaging teacher comments to come out of LHS - and one has a really terrific scholar and community citizen. Would add life and levity to JHU Engr w/ real intellectual rigor and talent. Enthusiastic admit/admit to BME for strong performance and yield tool.

3rd Comment (redacted title), 03/13/2013: ENG Comm(ittee): good for jhu good for bme; admit to both

Page 2 (a bunch of checkbox fields): No diploma GED Low grades Impressive essay Early grad Home school Failed Noteworthy Prev app Suspended Criminal CTY ROTC School Group HOME Ben dependent JH Employed Parent Fac/Staff support AO Noted VIP Follow VP? Peabody AO-Indicated FA (financial aid?) Baltimore Scholars BME decision

After the flood of FERPA requests, Yale and Stanford destroyed their records, which begs the question: What did they have to hide? Also interesting to note is that Hopkins took 2 emails and well over 45 days to get back to me. What took them so long? Were they “preparing” my documents for me?
